title: senna reniformis
intro: Senna reniformis, also known as Cape senna, is a plant species in the legume family, native to southern Africa. The medicinal properties of Senna reniformis lie in its laxative and purgative effects, due to the presence of anthraquinone glycosides. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including constipation, indigestion, and liver and gallbladder problems, as well as to promote digestion and relieve symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome. Senna reniformis can be prepared as an herbal tea or used as a tincture, with caution recommended due to its potential for dependency and interactions with other medications.
